prevalence.trend: increasing recognition; risk rises with social isolation and multimorbidity

burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; elevated suicide and medical comorbidity risk

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11 depression criteria; screening tools (GDS/PHQ-9) with careful differentiation from dementia and medical causes

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; improves with integrated care and psychotherapy access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS recognition variable; CAPS/geriatrics for complex cases
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Psychotherapy when available, SSRIs with geriatric considerations, treat pain and social factors, suicide risk management
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited geriatric mental health services and social support resources
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Primary care and CAPS can provide follow-up and access to core antidepressants
